summary:
Claim: Chief cells are located in the neck region of gastric glands.  
Evidence: - Functional advantage: Their location near the gland’s opening allows immediate release of pepsinogen into the lumen when stimulated by gastrin. - Histological confirmation: Chief cells form distinct clusters specifically in the neck zone of gastric glands.  
Answer: C
